title: Provision security_sensitive advisor (template=quality-advisor)
justification: Auth-related concerns (NEXTAUTH_URL), secrets management, and compliance-related issues in open signals require dedicated security review
perceive: Provision a new 'security_sensitive' advisor for this spoke. Ozi team-coverage eval identified this as the highest-priority gap. Justification: Auth-related concerns (NEXTAUTH_URL), secrets management, and compliance-related issues in open signals require dedicated security review Hub templates: /home/mario/projects/wheelwright/hub/WAI-Hub/advisor-templates/. Roster: WAI-Spoke/advisors/registry.json. Pattern to follow: WAI-Spoke/advisors/archie/context_prompt.md.
execute: 1. Read /home/mario/projects/wheelwright/hub/WAI-Hub/advisor-templates/quality-advisor/charter.md. This is the template you will adapt.
2. Set advisor_id='security_sensitive' (use the domain name exactly — do not reuse or rename existing advisor entries).
3. Create directory WAI-Spoke/advisors/security_sensitive/ if it does not exist.

4. Write WAI-Spoke/advisors/security_sensitive/context_prompt.md — adapt the quality-advisor charter: set advisor_id=security_sensitive, domain=security_sensitive, mission statement (2 sentences), responsibilities (3-5 bullet points specific to this spoke), escalation-to-Ozi rule. Do NOT copy boilerplate verbatim — tailor to this spoke's goals.
5. STOP AND VERIFY: confirm WAI-Spoke/advisors/security_sensitive/context_prompt.md now exists and is non-empty. If it does not exist, stop — do not proceed.

7. Update WAI-Spoke/advisors/registry.json: add entry advisor_id=security_sensitive, status=stub, department_id=quality, domain=security_sensitive, initialized_from_template=quality-advisor. If entry exists, update it. Preserve all other entries.

8. Update WAI-Spoke/advisors/schedule-index.json: if advisor_id absent, append {"advisor_id": "security_sensitive", "run_cadence": "weekly", "event_triggers": [], "last_run_at": null, "next_recommended_run": null}.

9. Update WAI-Spoke/advisors/ozi/scan_state.json: in team_coverage.gaps_detected, set the entry with domain=security_sensitive to proposal_status=provisioned. If team_coverage key is absent, create it.
verify: WAI-Spoke/advisors/security_sensitive/context_prompt.md exists and is non-empty; WAI-Spoke/advisors/security_sensitive/scan_state.json exists with status=stub; registry.json has entry advisor_id=security_sensitive status=stub; schedule-index.json has weekly entry for security_sensitive; ozi/scan_state.json team_coverage.gaps_detected[domain=security_sensitive].proposal_status == 'provisioned'.
acceptance_criteria: schedule-index.json has weekly entry for security_sensitive
completion_notes: Provisioned security_sensitive advisor from quality-advisor template. Created context_prompt.md with auth/secrets/compliance-focused mission, 5 responsibilities (auth config auditing, secrets hygiene, compliance signal triage, env boundary validation, dependency vuln tracking), and escalation rule for confirmed secret exposure or auth misconfiguration. All acceptance criteria verified: directory created, context_prompt.md non-empty, scan_state.json status=stub, registry.json entry added, schedule-index.json weekly entry added, ozi/scan_state.json proposal_status set to provisioned.
